extraction.one_line_summary: GILBERT Philippe won from a quality group sprint after KWIATKOWSKI Michal's 300m attack faded, decisive selection made on Cauberg.
tactical_patterns: KWIATKOWSKI attacked 300m out with huge gap but died spectacularly, revealing sprint timing beats explosive power here.
WELLENS Tim went solo 32km out without followers, suggesting peloton comfortable letting local riders burn matches early.
Quality selection happened on Cauberg with HAAS Nathan and ALBASINI missing turns, showing wheel positioning trumps pure climbing.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: negative: sailed away from group easily then completely emptied in final 100m, poor sprint management
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: timed sprint perfectly to overhaul fading attacker, fourth Amstel win confirms course mastery
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: aggressive 32km solo attempt in home region failed, national jersey didn't inspire tactical fear
rider_signals.KREUZIGER Roman: negative: crashed with 32km remaining alongside GASPAROTTO, suggesting positioning issues on technical sections
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: dropped on Cauberg despite being in quality group, hills too steep for classics engine
rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: negative: missed wheel in decisive move despite being present, lacking experience in chaotic finales
rider_signals.ALBASINI Michael: neutral: third place but missed key turn earlier, opportunistic rather than controlling
